---
title: "trt_het_wass_vignette"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(SuperLearner)
library(quantreg)
```


```{r}
# WASS INFERENCE START 
# Gist: need a command which takes X, A, Y vectors, returns the following:
# the heterogineity decomposition, estimated Psi^2 wasserstein effect across 
# covariates, testing for each term(?) [READ THE TESTING SECTION!]

# Helper, makes design matrix
make_design_matrix <- function(X, add_intercept = TRUE) {
  if (is.data.frame(X)) {
    non_numeric <- !vapply(X, is.numeric, logical(1))
    if (any(non_numeric)) {
      stop(
        "X must be numeric. Expand factors and construct nonlinear bases ",
        "before passing X to this function."
      )
    }
  }
  
  X <- as.matrix(X)
  storage.mode(X) <- "double"

  if (length(X) == 0L || nrow(X) == 0L) {
    stop("X must contain at least one observation.")
  }

  if (any(!is.finite(X))) {
    stop("X contains non-finite values.")
  }

  if (is.null(colnames(X))) {
    colnames(X) <- paste0("x", seq_len(ncol(X)))
  }

  if (add_intercept) {
    X <- cbind("(Intercept)" = rep(1, nrow(X)), X)
  }

  X
}

# For checking u \in (0,1)
make_u_grid <- function(trim = 0.05, n_grid = 1000) {
  if (!is.numeric(trim) || length(trim) != 1 || trim < 0 || trim >= 1) {
    stop("trim must be a scalar in [0, 1).")
  }

  if (!is.numeric(n_grid) || length(n_grid) != 1 || n_grid < 3) {
    stop("n_grid must be at least 3.")
  }

  seq(trim / 2, 1 - trim / 2, length.out = n_grid)
}


# Riemann rectangle widths 
trapz_weights <- function(u) {
  if (any(u <= 0) || any(u >= 1) || any(diff(u) <= 0)) {
    stop("u must be strictly increasing and lie inside (0, 1).")
  }

  M <- length(u)
  w <- numeric(M)

  w[1] <- u[1] + 0.5 * (u[2] - u[1])
  w[M] <- (1 - u[M]) + 0.5 * (u[M] - u[M - 1])

  if (M > 2) {
    w[2:(M - 1)] <- 0.5 * (u[3:M] - u[1:(M - 2)])
  }

  w / sum(w)
}

# Grid integral approximation, takes width (w) and height (H)
integrate_u <- function(H, w) {
  H <- as.matrix(H)
  rowSums(sweep(H, 2, w, "*"))
}

standard_error_from_if <- function(influence_values) {
  influence_values <- as.numeric(influence_values)

  if (length(influence_values) < 2L) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }

  sqrt(stats::var(influence_values) / length(influence_values))
}
```


```{r}
# Converts factor variable to the desired form
coerce_treatment <- function(A, treated_level = NULL) {
  original <- A

  if (is.logical(A)) {
    A01 <- as.integer(A)
    levels_used <- c(FALSE, TRUE)
    return(list(A = A01, treated_level = TRUE, control_level = FALSE))
  }

  if (is.numeric(A) && all(na.omit(unique(A)) %in% c(0, 1))) {
    return(list(A = as.integer(A), treated_level = 1, control_level = 0))
  }

  A_fac <- factor(A)

  if (nlevels(A_fac) != 2) {
    stop("A must have exactly two treatment levels.")
  }

  lev <- levels(A_fac)

  if (is.null(treated_level)) {
    treated_level <- lev[2]
  }

  if (!treated_level %in% lev) {
    stop("treated_level must be one of the observed treatment levels.")
  }

  A01 <- as.integer(A_fac == treated_level)
  control_level <- setdiff(lev, treated_level)

  list(
    A = A01,
    treated_level = treated_level,
    control_level = control_level,
    original = original
  )
}

```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Monotone rearrangement of quantile curves
#
# For a fixed x, the rearranged curve is the quantile function of
# Q_hat(U | x), where U is approximately Uniform(0,1).
#
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

rearrange_quantile_matrix <- function(Q) {
  Q <- as.matrix(Q)

  t(apply(Q, 1, sort))
}

```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Conditional density estimation at Q_a(u | x)
#
# Since dQ(u | x)/du = 1 / f(Q(u | x) | x), estimate density by a finite
# difference approximation to the derivative of the rearranged quantile curve.
#
# A slope floor is required because a finite grid and rearrangement can produce
# flat local stretches. This is a numerical regularization parameter and should
# be included in sensitivity analysis.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

density_from_quantile_grid <- function(Q, u, slope_floor = 1e-6) {
  Q <- as.matrix(Q)
  M <- length(u)

  dQ_du <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = nrow(Q), ncol = M)

  dQ_du[, 1] <- (Q[, 2] - Q[, 1]) / (u[2] - u[1])
  dQ_du[, M] <- (Q[, M] - Q[, M - 1]) / (u[M] - u[M - 1])

  if (M > 2) {
    for (m in 2:(M - 1)) {
      dQ_du[, m] <- (Q[, m + 1] - Q[, m - 1]) /
        (u[m + 1] - u[m - 1])
    }
  }

  dQ_du <- pmax(dQ_du, slope_floor)

  1 / dQ_du
}

```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Default nuisance estimators
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

fit_quantile_grid <- function(X, Y, u, method = "br") {
  X <- as.matrix(X)
  Y <- as.numeric(Y)

  B <- vapply(
    u,
    function(uu) {
      fit <- quantreg::rq.fit(
        x = X,
        y = Y,
        tau = uu,
        method = method
      )

      as.numeric(fit$coefficients)
    },
    numeric(ncol(X))
  )

  list(coef = t(B), u = u)
}


predict_quantile_grid <- function(fit, X_new) {
  as.matrix(X_new) %*% t(fit$coef)
}


as_sl_data <- function(X) {
  X <- as.data.frame(X)
  names(X) <- make.names(names(X), unique = TRUE)
  X
}


sl_predict_numeric <- function(fit, X_new) {
  pred <- predict(fit, newdata = as_sl_data(X_new), onlySL = TRUE)$pred
  as.numeric(pred)
}


default_superlearner_nuisance <- function(
    SL.library = c("SL.mean", "SL.glm", "SL.glm.interaction")) {

  list(
    fit_propensity = function(X, A) {
      SuperLearner::SuperLearner(
        Y = A,
        X = as_sl_data(X),
        family = binomial(),
        SL.library = SL.library
      )
    },

    predict_propensity = function(fit, X_new) {
      sl_predict_numeric(fit, X_new)
    },

    fit_mean = function(X, Y) {
      SuperLearner::SuperLearner(
        Y = Y,
        X = as_sl_data(X),
        family = gaussian(),
        SL.library = SL.library
      )
    },

    predict_mean = function(fit, X_new) {
      sl_predict_numeric(fit, X_new)
    },

    fit_cate = function(X, pseudo_outcome) {
      SuperLearner::SuperLearner(
        Y = pseudo_outcome,
        X = as_sl_data(X),
        family = gaussian(),
        SL.library = SL.library
      )
    },

    predict_cate = function(fit, X_new) {
      sl_predict_numeric(fit, X_new)
    },

    fit_quantiles = function(X, Y, u) {
      fit_quantile_grid(X, Y, u)
    },

    predict_quantiles = function(fit, X_new) {
      predict_quantile_grid(fit, X_new)
    }
  )
}

```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fold construction
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

make_stratified_folds <- function(A, K = 5L, seed = NULL) {
  A <- as.integer(A)

  if (!all(A %in% c(0L, 1L))) {
    stop("A must be coded 0 and 1.")
  }

  if (!is.null(seed)) {
    set.seed(seed)
  }

  group_sizes <- table(A)

  if (K < 2L || min(group_sizes) < K) {
    stop(
      "K must be at least 2 and cannot exceed the number of observations ",
      "in either treatment arm."
    )
  }

  fold <- integer(length(A))

  for (a in c(0L, 1L)) {
    index <- which(A == a)

    fold[index] <- sample(
      rep(seq_len(K), length.out = length(index)),
      size = length(index),
      replace = FALSE
    )
  }

  fold
}
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Quantile scores eta_a(Z, u)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

outcome_indicator_matrix <- function(Y, Q) {
  Q <- as.matrix(Q)

  if (length(Y) != nrow(Q)) {
    stop("Y and Q have incompatible dimensions.")
  }

  sweep(
    Q,
    MARGIN = 1L,
    STATS = Y,
    FUN = function(q, y) as.integer(y <= q)
  )
}

compute_eta <- function(A,
                        Y,
                        u,
                        Q1_hat,
                        Q0_hat,
                        pi_hat,
                        f1_hat,
                        f0_hat) {
  n <- length(Y)
  M <- length(u)

  U_mat <- matrix(
    u,
    nrow = n,
    ncol = M,
    byrow = TRUE
  )

  indicator_1 <- outcome_indicator_matrix(Y, Q1_hat)
  indicator_0 <- outcome_indicator_matrix(Y, Q0_hat)

  eta1 <- sweep(
    (U_mat - indicator_1) / f1_hat,
    MARGIN = 1L,
    STATS = as.numeric(A == 1L) / pi_hat,
    FUN = "*"
  )

  eta0 <- sweep(
    (U_mat - indicator_0) / f0_hat,
    MARGIN = 1L,
    STATS = as.numeric(A == 0L) / (1 - pi_hat),
    FUN = "*"
  )

  list(
    eta1 = eta1,
    eta0 = eta0,
    eta_diff = eta1 - eta0
  )
}
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Cross-fitted nuisance estimation
#
# The returned object contains out-of-fold predictions for every observation.
# Those predictions are then used by all estimators below.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

crossfit_wasserstein_nuisance <- function(
    X,
    A,
    Y,
    u = NULL,
    trim = 0.05,
    n_grid = 1000,
    K = 5,
    treated_level = NULL,
    folds = NULL,
    nuisance_fns = default_superlearner_nuisance(),
    rearrange = TRUE,
    slope_floor = 1e-6,
    propensity_floor = 0.0001,
    add_intercept = TRUE,
    seed = NULL) {

  if (!requireNamespace("SuperLearner", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'SuperLearner' is required for the default nuisance learners.")
  }

  if (!requireNamespace("quantreg", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'quantreg' is required for the default quantile learner.")
  }

  if (!is.null(seed)) {
  set.seed(seed)
}

  if (is.null(u)) {
    u <- make_u_grid(trim = trim, n_grid = n_grid)
  }

  w <- trapz_weights(u)

  X <- make_design_matrix(X, add_intercept = add_intercept)
  Y <- as.numeric(Y)

  A_info <- coerce_treatment(A, treated_level = treated_level)
  A <- A_info$A

  n <- nrow(X)
  M <- length(u)

  if (length(Y) != n || length(A) != n) {
   stop("X, A, and Y have incompatible dimensions.")
  }

  if (is.null(folds)) {
    folds <- make_stratified_folds(A = A, K = K, seed = seed)
  }
  pi_hat <- numeric(n)
  mu1_hat <- numeric(n)
  mu0_hat <- numeric(n)

  Q1_hat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = M)
  Q0_hat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = n, ncol = M)

  for (k in seq_len(K)) {
    test <- which(folds == k)
    train <- which(folds != k)

    X_train <- X[train, , drop = FALSE]
    X_test <- X[test, , drop = FALSE]
    A_train <- A[train]
    Y_train <- Y[train]

    # Propensity
    pi_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_propensity(X_train, A_train)
    pi_hat[test] <- nuisance_fns$predict_propensity(pi_fit, X_test)

    # Outcome regressions
    mu1_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_mean(
      X_train[A_train == 1, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 1]
    )

    mu0_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_mean(
      X_train[A_train == 0, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 0]
    )

    mu1_hat[test] <- nuisance_fns$predict_mean(mu1_fit, X_test)
    mu0_hat[test] <- nuisance_fns$predict_mean(mu0_fit, X_test)

    # Conditional quantiles
    q1_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_quantiles(
      X_train[A_train == 1, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 1],
      u
    )

    q0_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_quantiles(
      X_train[A_train == 0, , drop = FALSE],
      Y_train[A_train == 0],
      u
    )

    q1 <- nuisance_fns$predict_quantiles(q1_fit, X_test)
    q0 <- nuisance_fns$predict_quantiles(q0_fit, X_test)

    if (rearrange) {
      q1 <- rearrange_quantile_matrix(q1)
      q0 <- rearrange_quantile_matrix(q0)
    }

    Q1_hat[test, ] <- q1
    Q0_hat[test, ] <- q0
  }

  if (any(pi_hat < propensity_floor | pi_hat > 1 - propensity_floor)) {
    warning(
      "Some estimated propensity scores are outside [",
      propensity_floor, ", ", 1 - propensity_floor,
      "]. They will be truncated. Check positivity."
    )
  }

  pi_hat <- pmin(pmax(pi_hat, propensity_floor), 1 - propensity_floor)

  R_tau <- A / pi_hat * (Y - mu1_hat) -
    (1 - A) / (1 - pi_hat) * (Y - mu0_hat)

  pseudo_tau <- mu1_hat - mu0_hat + R_tau

  tau_x_hat <- numeric(n)

  for (k in seq_len(K)) {
    test <- which(folds == k)
    train <- which(folds != k)

    cate_fit <- nuisance_fns$fit_cate(
      X[train, , drop = FALSE],
      pseudo_tau[train]
    )

    tau_x_hat[test] <- nuisance_fns$predict_cate(
      cate_fit,
      X[test, , drop = FALSE]
    )
  }

  f1_hat <- density_from_quantile_grid(
    Q = Q1_hat,
    u = u,
    slope_floor = slope_floor
  )

  f0_hat <- density_from_quantile_grid(
    Q = Q0_hat,
    u = u,
    slope_floor = slope_floor
  )

  eta <- compute_eta(
    A = A,
    Y = Y,
    u = u,
    Q1_hat = Q1_hat,
    Q0_hat = Q0_hat,
    pi_hat = pi_hat,
    f1_hat = f1_hat,
    f0_hat = f0_hat
  )

  delta_hat <- Q1_hat - Q0_hat
  bar_delta_hat <- colMeans(delta_hat)

  structure(
    list(
      X = X,
      A = A,
      Y = Y,
      treated_level = A_info$treated_level,
      control_level = A_info$control_level,

      u = u,
      w = w,
      folds = folds,

      pi_hat = pi_hat,
      mu1_hat = mu1_hat,
      mu0_hat = mu0_hat,
      R_tau = R_tau,
      pseudo_tau = pseudo_tau,
      tau_x_hat = tau_x_hat,
      tau_hat = mean(pseudo_tau),

      Q1_hat = Q1_hat,
      Q0_hat = Q0_hat,
      delta_hat = delta_hat,
      bar_delta_hat = bar_delta_hat,

      f1_hat = f1_hat,
      f0_hat = f0_hat,
      eta1 = eta$eta1,
      eta0 = eta$eta0,
      eta_diff = eta$eta_diff,

      slope_floor = slope_floor,
      propensity_floor = propensity_floor
    ),
    class = "wasserstein_cf"
  )
}
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Term 1: Conditional Wasserstein effect psi^2(X_i)
#
# For the observed X_i, this returns the cross-fitted estimate
#
#   psi_hat^2(X_i) = sum_m w_m Delta_hat(X_i, u_m)^2.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Conditional Wasserstein effect
estimate_psi2_x <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_cf"))

  integrate_u(cf$delta_hat^2, cf$w)
}


# Average Wasserstein effect

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Overall Wasserstein effect Psi^2
#
# Psi_hat^2 =
#   n^{-1} sum_i int [
#     Delta_hat(X_i,u)^2 +
#     2 Delta_hat(X_i,u) {eta_hat_1(Z_i,u) - eta_hat_0(Z_i,u)}
#   ] du.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_Psi2 <- function(cf) {
  delta <- cf$delta_hat

  plug_i <- integrate_u(delta^2, cf$w)

  correction_i <- 2 * integrate_u(
    delta * cf$eta_diff,
    cf$w
  )

  contribution <- plug_i + correction_i
  est <- mean(contribution)

  list(
    estimate = est,
    plug_in = mean(plug_i),
    correction = mean(correction_i),
    contribution = contribution,
    se = sd(contribution) / sqrt(length(contribution))
  )
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# V[tau(X)]
#
# theta_a = E[tau^2(X)]
#
# DR one-step style estimator using the Kennedy-style pseudo-outcome:
#   phi_tau = mu1 - mu0 + A/pi(Y-mu1) - (1-A)/(1-pi)(Y-mu0)
#
# Since E[phi_tau | X] = tau(X), estimate E[tau^2(X)] by
#   tau_hat(X)^2 + 2 tau_hat(X) {phi_tau - tau_hat(X)}.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
estimate_tau2 <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_cf"))

  tau_hat <- cf$tau_hat

  influence <- 2 * tau_hat * (cf$pseudo_tau - tau_hat)

  list(
    estimate = tau_hat^2,
    tau_hat = tau_hat,
    influence = influence,
    se = standard_error_from_if(influence)
  )
}


estimate_V_tauX <- function(cf) {
  stopifnot(inherits(cf, "wasserstein_cf"))

  centered_tau_x <- cf$tau_x_hat - cf$tau_hat

  contribution <- centered_tau_x^2 +
    2 * centered_tau_x * (cf$pseudo_tau - cf$tau_x_hat)

  estimate <- mean(contribution)

  list(
    estimate = estimate,
    contribution = contribution,
    influence = contribution - estimate,
    se = standard_error_from_if(contribution - estimate)
  )
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# theta_b = E[b^2(U)]
# b(U) = bar_Delta(U) - tau.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
estimate_theta_b <- function(cf) {

  b_hat <- cf$bar_delta_hat - cf$tau_hat

  plug <- sum(cf$w * b_hat^2)

  eta_correction_i <- 2 * integrate_u(
    sweep(cf$eta_diff, 2, b_hat, "*"),
    cf$w
  )

  tau_correction_i <- -2 * sum(cf$w * b_hat) *
    (cf$pseudo_tau - cf$tau_hat)

  contribution <- plug + eta_correction_i + tau_correction_i

  list(
    estimate = mean(contribution),
    plug_in = plug,
    correction = mean(eta_correction_i + tau_correction_i),
    b_hat = b_hat,
    contribution = contribution,
    se = sd(contribution) / sqrt(length(contribution))
  )
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# # theta_c = E[c^2(X, U)]
# c(X, U) = Delta(X, U) - tau(X) - b(U)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_theta_c <- function(cf) {
  # theta_c = E int c^2(X,u) du
  # c(X,u) = Delta(X,u) - tau(X) - b(u)

  b_hat <- cf$bar_delta_hat - cf$tau_hat

  c_hat <- sweep(cf$delta_hat, 1, cf$tau_x_hat, "-")
  c_hat <- sweep(c_hat, 2, b_hat, "-")

  plug_i <- integrate_u(c_hat^2, cf$w)

  eta_correction_i <- 2 * integrate_u(
    c_hat * cf$eta_diff,
    cf$w
  )

  tau_x_correction_i <- -2 * integrate_u(c_hat, cf$w) *
    (cf$pseudo_tau - cf$tau_x_hat)

  contribution <- plug_i + eta_correction_i + tau_x_correction_i

  list(
    estimate = mean(contribution),
    plug_in = mean(plug_i),
    correction = mean(eta_correction_i + tau_x_correction_i),
    c_hat = c_hat,
    contribution = contribution,
    se = sd(contribution) / sqrt(length(contribution))
  )
}


# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Full one-step decomposition
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

estimate_wasserstein_decomposition <- function(cf) {
  Psi2 <- estimate_Psi2(cf)
  tau2 <- estimate_tau2(cf)
  V_tauX <- estimate_V_tauX(cf)
  theta_b <- estimate_theta_b(cf)
  theta_c <- estimate_theta_c(cf)

  decomposition_sum <- (
    tau2$estimate +
      V_tauX$estimate +
      theta_b$estimate +
      theta_c$estimate
  )

  list(
    Psi2 = Psi2,
    tau2 = tau2,
    V_tauX = V_tauX,
    theta_b = theta_b,
    theta_c = theta_c,
    decomposition_sum = decomposition_sum,
    decomposition_gap = Psi2$estimate - decomposition_sum,
    tau_hat = cf$tau_hat
  )
}
```





```{r}
# FOR EXAMPLE IMPLEMENTATION, MAKES estimate_example FUNCTION 

library(SuperLearner)
library(quantreg)

set.seed(2026)

n <- 3000

estimate_example <- function(dat, n_grid = 99, trim = 0.05) {
  X_mat <- model.matrix(~ factor(X), data = dat)

  cf <- crossfit_wasserstein_nuisance(
    X = X_mat,
    A = dat$A,
    Y = dat$Y,
    trim = trim,
    n_grid = n_grid,
    K = 5,
    add_intercept = FALSE,
    treated_level = "treated",
    nuisance_fns = default_superlearner_nuisance(
      SL.library = c("SL.mean", "SL.glm", "SL.glm.interaction")
    ),
    rearrange = TRUE,
    seed = 2026
  )

  fit <- estimate_wasserstein_decomposition(cf)

  data.frame(
  term = c(
    "Psi2",
    "tau2",
    "V_tauX",
    "theta_b",
    "theta_c",
    "decomposition_sum",
    "decomposition_gap"
  ),
  estimate = c(
    fit$Psi2$estimate,
    fit$tau2$estimate,
    fit$V_tauX$estimate,
    fit$theta_b$estimate,
    fit$theta_c$estimate,
    fit$decomposition_sum,
    fit$decomposition_gap
  )
)
}



```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 1 SIMULTATIONS

X <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE)

A <- factor(
  rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5),
  levels = c(0, 1),
  labels = c("control", "treated")
)

mu0 <- c(20, 30, 40)
sd0 <- c(1, 1, 1)

mu1 <- c(30, 40, 50)
sd1 <- c(1, 1, 1)

Y <- rnorm(
  n,
  mean = ifelse(A == "treated", mu1[X], mu0[X]),
  sd = ifelse(A == "treated", sd1[X], sd0[X])
)

dat1 <- data.frame(X = X, A = A, Y = Y)

case1_results <- estimate_example(dat1)
case1_results

```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 2 SIMULATION

X <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE)

A <- factor(
  rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5),
  levels = c(0, 1),
  labels = c("control", "treated")
)

mu0 <- c(20, 30, 40)
sd0 <- c(1, 1, 1)

# Mean shifts: -2, 0, 2
# Standard deviation changes: 8, 8, 8
# Example 2
mu1 <- c(16, 30, 44)
sd1 <- c(4, 4, 4)

Y <- rnorm(
  n,
  mean = ifelse(A == "treated", mu1[X], mu0[X]),
  sd = ifelse(A == "treated", sd1[X], sd0[X])
)

dat2 <- data.frame(X = X, A = A, Y = Y)

case2_results <- estimate_example(dat2)
case2_results
```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 3 SIMULATION
X <- sample(1:3, size = n, replace = TRUE)

A <- factor(
  rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5),
  levels = c(0, 1),
  labels = c("control", "treated")
)

mu0 <- c(20, 30, 40)
sd0 <- c(1, 1, 1)

# Mean shifts: -2, 0, 2
# Standard deviation changes: 0, 12, 0
mu1 <- c(16, 30, 44)
sd1 <- c(1, 7, 1)

Y <- rnorm(
  n,
  mean = ifelse(A == "treated", mu1[X], mu0[X]),
  sd = ifelse(A == "treated", sd1[X], sd0[X])
)

dat3 <- data.frame(X = X, A = A, Y = Y)

case3_results <- estimate_example(dat3)
case3_results

```




```{r}
# EXAMPLE 1 VISUAL

library(plotly)

set.seed(123)



n_per_group <- 200

species <- rep(1:3, each = 2 * n_per_group)

soil_type <- rep(
  rep(c(1, 0), each = n_per_group),
  times = 3
)

p_11 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)
p_10 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 1)

p_21 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)
p_20 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)

p_31 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 50, sd = 1)
p_30 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)

p <- c(
  p_11, p_10,
  p_21, p_20,
  p_31, p_30
)

dat <- data.frame(
  species = factor(species),
  soil_type = factor(soil_type),
  p = p
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Horizontal positions:
# large gaps between species, small gaps within species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_x <- c("1" = 1, "2" = 4, "3" = 7)

soil_offset <- c(
  "0" = -0.28,
  "1" =  0.28
)

soil_colors <- c(
  "0" = "royalblue",
  "1" = "red"
)

# Use one common support grid so curves are directly comparable
p_grid <- seq(
  min(dat$p) - 2,
  max(dat$p) + 2,
  length.out = 250
)

fig <- plot_ly()

for (sp in levels(dat$species)) {
  for (soil in levels(dat$soil_type)) {

    values <- dat$p[
      dat$species == sp &
      dat$soil_type == soil
    ]

    dens <- density(
      values,
      from = min(p_grid),
      to = max(p_grid),
      n = length(p_grid)
    )

    x_location <- species_x[sp] + soil_offset[soil]

    # 3D density curve
    fig <- fig %>%
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = dens$y,
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 8
        ),
        name = paste("Soil", soil),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = (sp == "1"),
        hovertemplate = paste0(
          "Species: ", sp,
          "<br>Soil type: ", soil,
          "<br>Outcome: %{y:.2f}",
          "<br>Density: %{z:.3f}",
          "<extra></extra>"
        )
      ) %>%
      # Optional baseline for locating each curve in 3D space
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = rep(0, length(dens$x)),
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 2,
          dash = "dot"
        ),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = FALSE,
        hoverinfo = "skip"
      )
  }
}

fig %>%
  layout(
    title = "Distributional Curves by Species and Soil Type",
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Soil type")
    ),
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(
        title = "X",
        tickvals = unname(species_x),
        ticktext = names(species_x),
        range = c(0, 8),
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Y",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      zaxis = list(
        title = "Density",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = -1.7, z = 1.1)
      )
    )
  )

```


```{r}
# EXAMPLE 2 VISUAL
library(plotly)

set.seed(123)



n_per_group <- 200

species <- rep(1:3, each = 2 * n_per_group)

soil_type <- rep(
  rep(c(1, 0), each = n_per_group),
  times = 3
)

# Treated distributions: variance = 4, so sd = 2
p_11 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 10, sd = 3)
p_21 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 3)
p_31 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 50, sd = 3)

# Untreated distributions unchanged: sd = 1
p_10 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 1)
p_20 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)
p_30 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)

p <- c(
  p_11, p_10,
  p_21, p_20,
  p_31, p_30
)

dat <- data.frame(
  species = factor(species),
  soil_type = factor(soil_type),
  p = p
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# x-axis spacing:
# bigger gaps between species, smaller gaps within species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_x <- c("1" = 1, "2" = 4, "3" = 7)
soil_offset <- c("0" = -0.28, "1" = 0.28)

soil_colors <- c(
  "0" = "royalblue",
  "1" = "red"
)

# Common support for all density curves
p_grid <- seq(
  min(dat$p) - 2,
  max(dat$p) + 2,
  length.out = 1000
)

fig <- plot_ly()

for (sp in levels(dat$species)) {
  for (soil in levels(dat$soil_type)) {
    
    values <- dat$p[
      dat$species == sp &
      dat$soil_type == soil
    ]
    
    # Smooth kernel density estimate
    dens <- density(
      values,
      from = min(p_grid),
      to = max(p_grid),
      n = 1000,
      adjust = 1.4
    )
    
    x_location <- species_x[sp] + soil_offset[soil]
    
    # Main 3D density curve
    fig <- fig %>%
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = dens$y,
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 8
        ),
        name = paste("Soil", soil),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = (sp == "1"),
        hovertemplate = paste0(
          "Species: ", sp,
          "<br>Soil type: ", soil,
          "<br>Outcome: %{y:.2f}",
          "<br>Density: %{z:.3f}",
          "<extra></extra>"
        )
      ) %>%
      # Baseline beneath each curve
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = rep(0, length(dens$x)),
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 2,
          dash = "dot"
        ),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = FALSE,
        hoverinfo = "skip"
      )
  }
}

fig %>%
  layout(
    title = "Smoothed Distributional Curves by Species and Soil Type",
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Soil type")
    ),
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(
        title = "X",
        tickvals = unname(species_x),
        ticktext = names(species_x),
        range = c(0, 8),
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Y",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      zaxis = list(
        title = "Density",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = -1.7, z = 1.1)
      )
    )
  )
```

```{r}
# EXAMPLE 3 VISUAL
library(plotly)

set.seed(123)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulate:
# 3 species × 2 soil types × 200 observations = 1200 total
#
# Soil type 1 = treated (red)
# Soil type 0 = untreated (blue)
#
# Treated variance = 4  => treated SD = 2
# Treated means: 10, 30, 50
#
# Untreated distributions remain the same:
# means: 10, 20, 30
# SD = 1
# ------------------------------------------------------------

n_per_group <- 200

species <- rep(1:3, each = 2 * n_per_group)

soil_type <- rep(
  rep(c(1, 0), each = n_per_group),
  times = 3
)

# Treated distributions: variance = 4, so sd = 2
p_11 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 10, sd = 1)
p_21 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 2)
p_31 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 50, sd = 3)

# Untreated distributions unchanged: sd = 1
p_10 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 20, sd = 1)
p_20 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 30, sd = 1)
p_30 <- rnorm(n_per_group, mean = 40, sd = 1)

p <- c(
  p_11, p_10,
  p_21, p_20,
  p_31, p_30
)

dat <- data.frame(
  species = factor(species),
  soil_type = factor(soil_type),
  p = p
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# x-axis spacing:
# bigger gaps between species, smaller gaps within species
# ------------------------------------------------------------

species_x <- c("1" = 1, "2" = 4, "3" = 7)
soil_offset <- c("0" = -0.28, "1" = 0.28)

soil_colors <- c(
  "0" = "royalblue",
  "1" = "red"
)

# Common support for all density curves
p_grid <- seq(
  min(dat$p) - 2,
  max(dat$p) + 2,
  length.out = 1000
)

fig <- plot_ly()

for (sp in levels(dat$species)) {
  for (soil in levels(dat$soil_type)) {
    
    values <- dat$p[
      dat$species == sp &
      dat$soil_type == soil
    ]
    
    # Smooth kernel density estimate
    dens <- density(
      values,
      from = min(p_grid),
      to = max(p_grid),
      n = 1000,
      adjust = 1.4
    )
    
    x_location <- species_x[sp] + soil_offset[soil]
    
    # Main 3D density curve
    fig <- fig %>%
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = dens$y,
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 8
        ),
        name = paste("Soil", soil),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = (sp == "1"),
        hovertemplate = paste0(
          "Species: ", sp,
          "<br>Soil type: ", soil,
          "<br>Outcome: %{y:.2f}",
          "<br>Density: %{z:.3f}",
          "<extra></extra>"
        )
      ) %>%
      # Baseline beneath each curve
      add_trace(
        type = "scatter3d",
        mode = "lines",
        x = rep(x_location, length(dens$x)),
        y = dens$x,
        z = rep(0, length(dens$x)),
        line = list(
          color = soil_colors[soil],
          width = 2,
          dash = "dot"
        ),
        legendgroup = paste("Soil", soil),
        showlegend = FALSE,
        hoverinfo = "skip"
      )
  }
}

fig %>%
  layout(
    title = "Smoothed Distributional Curves by Species and Soil Type",
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Soil type")
    ),
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(
        title = "X",
        tickvals = unname(species_x),
        ticktext = names(species_x),
        range = c(0, 8),
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Y",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      zaxis = list(
        title = "Density",
        showgrid = FALSE,
        zeroline = FALSE,
        showbackground = FALSE
      ),
      camera = list(
        eye = list(x = 1.5, y = -1.7, z = 1.1)
      )
    )
  )
```


```{r}
# JOB CORPS EXAMPLE FOR DR. SCHINDL, DR. WASSERMAN
#install.packages('causalweight')
pkg <- "causalweight"
repo <- "https://cloud.r-project.org"
ap <- available.packages(repos = repo)
ver <- ap[pkg, "Version"]
tarball <- tempfile(fileext = ".tar.gz")
url <- sprintf("%s/src/contrib/%s_%s.tar.gz", repo, pkg, ver)
download.file(url, tarball, mode = "wb")
# Extract only the JC data file
exdir <- tempfile()
untar(
  tarball,
  files = sprintf("%s/data/JC.RData", pkg),
  exdir = exdir
)
# Load JC into your R environment
load(file.path(exdir, pkg, "data", "JC.RData"))
dim(JC)
names(JC)



dat <- JC

A <- dat$assignment
Y <- dat$earny4
X <- dat[, c("female", "age", "educ", "everwkd", "mwearn")]

dat_jc <- data.frame(X = X[,"everwkd", ], A, Y)
estimate_terms(dat_jc)
```
